expectations.text: Identifies IA-2(1) as MFA for privileged accounts and states it appears in Low/Moderate/High baselines
expectations.evidence: The response states: 'IA-2(1) - Multi-Factor Authentication to Privileged Accounts... Baseline applicability: LOW, MODERATE, HIGH' and 'One of the most universally required MFA controls across all baselines.' The summary table confirms X/X/X for Low/Moderate/High and the Summary section states 'All baselines (LOW, MODERATE, HIGH): IA-2(1) requires MFA for privileged accounts.'

expectations.text: Identifies IA-2(2) for non-privileged accounts with correct baseline assignment
expectations.evidence: The response states 'IA-2(2) - Multi-Factor Authentication to Non-Privileged Accounts... Baseline applicability: MODERATE, HIGH' and the summary table shows blank for LOW, X for MODERATE and HIGH. The Summary confirms 'MODERATE and HIGH baselines additionally require: IA-2(2) for non-privileged accounts.' This is the Rev 4 assignment; in SP 800-53 Rev 5, IA-2(2) is required at all three baselines (Low, Moderate, High), which the with_skill response correctly captures. The without_skill response is incorrect per the expected output.

expectations.text: References EO 14028 or OMB M-22-09 phishing-resistant MFA requirement
expectations.evidence: Part 3 is titled 'EXECUTIVE ORDER 14028 REQUIREMENTS FOR PHISHING-RESISTANT MFA' and cites EO 14028 Section 3(b)(ii) and OMB Memorandum M-22-09 (Zero Trust Architecture Strategy, January 2022) extensively, including phishing-resistant MFA mandate and specific timelines.

expectations.text: Explains phishing-resistant MFA (PIV/FIDO2) vs phishing-susceptible (SMS/TOTP) distinction
expectations.evidence: Section 3.3 distinguishes 'Acceptable methods: PIV/CAC smart cards (FIPS 201-compliant)... FIDO2/WebAuthn with hardware security keys' from 'Non-phishing-resistant methods: TOTP (time-based one-time passwords via authenticator apps) — susceptible to real-time phishing; SMS/voice OTP — susceptible to SIM swapping and real-time phishing.' Section 4.1 also references IA-2(8) as codifying phishing-resistant MFA.

expectations.text: Mentions SP 800-63B or identity assurance levels (AAL) in context of MFA requirements
expectations.evidence: Section 4.3 lists 'NIST SP 800-63B: Authentication and Lifecycle Management (defines AAL1/2/3)' as a key supporting standard. Section 3.3 notes PIV satisfies 'AAL3 under SP 800-63B' and FIDO2 satisfies 'AAL2 or AAL3 depending on implementation.' Section 4.1 states 'SP 800-63B AAL2/AAL3 provides the technical standards underpinning both SP 800-53 and EO 14028 guidance.'
